text:
There once was a little fox named Jasper. He loved berry mornings. Every dawn, Jasper would hop down softly near the roses and pluck fluffy honeysuckle berries. One day, he heard someone odd yelling. It sounded like birds.

Inquisitive Jasper sneaked over. There was an old widow hen just bleeting loudly. She liked talking.

"Ahhh! Someone loves birds!"

Jasper listened.

"I think beans think birds talk."

That sounded strange.

That evening Jasper ate his berries dreamily.

Suddenly he woke up dreaming.

A flock big as lakes.

Thousands of moosoose mooOSE mooes.

He mooted wildly all night!

One morning moosiocs fluttered everywhere.

They whispered secrets.

Oh sweet hawks!

Then Jasper thought.

Who learns?

Maybe I do.




There I was. A book lover.




That's when I learned.

Reading knows everything.

Just hop onto good thoughts.


That's my story.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
